name: Photopea
tagline: A full Photoshop-class image editor that runs entirely in your browser.
description: Photopea opens PSD, XCF, Sketch, and RAW files and offers layers, masks, smart objects, and filters — no download, no account. It is one of the strongest demonstrations of what a PWA can do, matching desktop software feature-for-feature.

name: Spotify Web Player
tagline: Stream Spotify's full music and podcast catalog without installing the desktop app.
description: The Spotify web player is an installable PWA with the full streaming catalog, playlists, and podcast support. It launches in its own window and integrates with system media keys.

name: Excalidraw
tagline: A hand-drawn-style virtual whiteboard for sketching diagrams and ideas.
description: Excalidraw is an open-source whiteboard with a distinctive sketchy aesthetic. It works fully offline, requires no account, supports real-time collaboration via shareable links, and exports to PNG and SVG.

name: YouTube Music
tagline: Google's music streaming service, installable straight from the browser.
description: YouTube Music runs as a first-class PWA with background playback in its own window, media-key support, and the full YouTube music catalog including live performances and remixes.

name: Pinterest
tagline: Visual discovery and bookmarking — one of the original PWA success stories.
description: Pinterest's PWA rebuild famously increased engagement by 60% and became a canonical case study. It offers the full pin, board, and discovery experience with push notifications and a fast, app-like feel.

name: Telegram Web
tagline: Full-featured Telegram messaging from any browser.
description: Telegram Web is a complete messaging client with chats, channels, media sharing, and push notifications. It syncs instantly with your phone and installs as a standalone app window.

name: VS Code for the Web
tagline: Visual Studio Code running fully in the browser, no install required.
description: vscode.dev brings the real VS Code editor to the browser: open local folders with the File System Access API, edit GitHub repos directly, and use many extensions. Installs as a desktop-class PWA.

name: Squoosh
tagline: Compress and convert images with instant side-by-side comparison.
description: Squoosh, built by the Chrome team, compresses images to WebP, AVIF, MozJPEG and more — entirely on-device, so nothing is uploaded. It works fully offline and weighs almost nothing.

name: Todoist
tagline: A polished cross-platform task manager with offline support.
description: Todoist's web app is an installable PWA with natural-language task entry, projects, labels, and reminders. Changes made offline sync automatically when you reconnect.

name: StackBlitz
tagline: Instant full-stack dev environments that boot in your browser.
description: StackBlitz runs Node.js inside the browser via WebContainers, so npm installs and dev servers work with no local setup. Great for prototyping, bug repros, and sharing runnable examples.

name: Uber
tagline: Request rides from a web app small enough to load on 2G.
description: Uber built m.uber.com so riders on low-end devices and slow networks could book without the native app. The core ride-request flow loads in seconds even on constrained connections.

name: Starbucks
tagline: Browse the menu and order ahead — even while offline.
description: The Starbucks PWA lets customers browse the menu, customize drinks, and build orders offline, syncing when a connection returns. It is roughly 99% smaller than the iOS app and doubled daily active web users.

name: Monkeytype
tagline: A minimalist, endlessly customizable typing test.
description: Monkeytype is the most popular typing practice site on the web, with themes, test modes, and detailed stats. It installs as a lightweight PWA and needs no account to use.

name: Proxx
tagline: A space-themed Minesweeper that runs on anything, even feature phones.
description: Proxx, built by the Chrome team as a PWA showcase, is a polished Minesweeper-style game that works offline and runs smoothly on everything from flagship phones to KaiOS feature phones.

name: YouTube
tagline: The world's largest video platform, installable as an app.
description: YouTube's site is a full PWA: install it for an app-like window with the complete video catalog, subscriptions, and playlists. Works with system media keys and picture-in-picture.

name: X (Twitter)
tagline: The full X/Twitter experience — the original 'Twitter Lite' PWA.
description: X's web app descends from Twitter Lite, one of the most influential PWAs ever shipped. Timeline, posting, DMs, and push notifications, in a fraction of the native app's size.

name: TikTok
tagline: Browse and post TikTok videos from an installable web app.
description: TikTok's website is an installable PWA with the full For You feed, search, and posting. Handy for watching without the native app's battery and data appetite.

name: GitHub
tagline: The world's largest code-hosting platform, installable from the browser.
description: GitHub ships a web app manifest, so you can install it and manage repositories, issues, and pull requests in a standalone window. Pairs well with vscode.dev for editing.

name: Duolingo
tagline: Gamified language lessons in an installable web app.
description: Duolingo's web app covers the full course catalog with streaks, leagues, and practice reminders. Installing the PWA gives you lessons in their own window without the mobile app.

name: SoundCloud
tagline: Stream independent music and podcasts from an installable player.
description: SoundCloud's web player installs as a PWA with the platform's entire catalog of independent artists, DJ sets, and podcasts, plus system media-key support.

name: Chess.com
tagline: Play chess online with millions of players, puzzles, and lessons.
description: Chess.com's web app is installable and offers live games, daily correspondence chess, tactics puzzles, and lessons — the complete experience without the native app.

name: Lichess
tagline: Free, open-source, ad-free online chess.
description: Lichess is a beloved open-source chess server: instant games, puzzles, studies, and tournaments with no ads and no account required. Fast, lightweight, and installable.

name: Replit
tagline: Write, run, and host code in dozens of languages from the browser.
description: Replit is a full development environment in a tab: editors, terminals, package management, and one-click hosting. Installing the PWA makes it feel like a desktop IDE.

name: diagrams.net (draw.io)
tagline: Free flowchart and diagram editor that works fully offline.
description: diagrams.net (formerly draw.io) is the standard free tool for flowcharts, network diagrams, and org charts. The PWA works completely offline and saves to local files or cloud drives without an account.

name: The Weather Channel
tagline: Forecasts, radar, and severe-weather alerts on the web.
description: weather.com is an installable PWA with local forecasts, interactive radar maps, and severe-weather alerts — one of the early large-scale PWA deployments.

name: Tinder
tagline: Tinder's swipe-to-match dating app on the web.
description: Tinder Online is a deliberate PWA case study: the swipe experience in a web app far smaller than the native download, with matches and messaging included.

name: Mastodon
tagline: Decentralized, open-source social network you can install.
description: Mastodon's web client is a PWA on every server; mastodon.social is the flagship instance run by the Mastodon nonprofit. Timelines, posting, and push notifications, no algorithmic feed.

name: Windy
tagline: Stunning animated wind, weather, and radar maps.
description: Windy visualizes wind, rain, temperature, and dozens of other layers on a beautiful animated globe. Loved by pilots, sailors, and storm watchers; installs as a PWA.

name: tldraw
tagline: A fast, minimal infinite-canvas whiteboard.
description: tldraw is an open-source infinite canvas for quick sketches and diagrams with real-time multiplayer. Instant to load, no account needed, installable as an app.

name: Pixlr
tagline: Quick photo editing and templates in the browser.
description: Pixlr offers layer-based photo editing and design templates aimed at fast social-media work. Lighter than Photopea for quick fixes, and installable as a PWA.

tagline: The original tile-sliding puzzle game by its creator.
description: Gabriele Cirulli's original 2048 — slide tiles, merge numbers, chase the 2048 tile. The official site is a tiny installable PWA, perfect for offline commutes.

name: Financial Times
tagline: The FT's pioneering offline-capable news web app.
description: app.ft.com is one of the earliest serious news PWAs: the Financial Times built it to escape app stores, with offline reading and fast load on any device. Subscription required for full access.

name: PWABuilder
tagline: Microsoft's tool for packaging PWAs for app stores.
description: PWABuilder analyzes your PWA, scores its manifest and service worker, and packages it for the Microsoft, Google, and Apple stores. Fittingly, it is itself an installable PWA.

name: Gmail
tagline: Google's email service, installable with offline support.
description: Gmail installs as a PWA with search, labels, and offline access to recent mail. Our automated verification can't see past its sign-in page, so it carries the at-your-own-risk label — check that the address is mail.google.com before signing in.

name: Instagram
tagline: Instagram's feed, stories, and DMs from an installable web app.
description: Instagram's website is installable and covers the feed, stories, search, and messaging. It loads its app manifest dynamically, which our automated verification can't observe.

name: WhatsApp Web
tagline: WhatsApp messaging paired to your phone, in the browser.
description: WhatsApp Web mirrors your phone's chats with full messaging, media, and calls in supported browsers. It refuses automated requests, so it can't be machine-verified — pair only at web.whatsapp.com.

name: Canva
tagline: Drag-and-drop design for presentations, posters, and social posts.
description: Canva's editor installs as a PWA with thousands of templates for social graphics, slides, and print. It blocks automated checks, so it carries the at-your-own-risk label.

name: Google Photos
tagline: Photo backup, search, and editing in an installable web app.
description: Google Photos works as a PWA for browsing, searching, and lightly editing your photo library. Automated verification can't see past its sign-in page.

name: Outlook
tagline: Microsoft's email and calendar as an installable web app.
description: Outlook on the web installs as a PWA with mail, calendar, and contacts. Like Gmail, it hides everything behind sign-in, so automated verification can't inspect it.

name: Google Meet
tagline: Video meetings straight from the browser, no download.
description: Google Meet runs entirely on the web and installs as a PWA for quick access to calls. Automated verification can't see past its account gate.

name: Bluesky
tagline: The open-protocol social network, installable from bsky.app.
description: Bluesky's web client is installable and covers the full experience: feeds, posting, and custom algorithms on the AT Protocol. Its manifest loads via scripts our checker can't run.

name: Flipkart
tagline: India's biggest online store — home of the famous Flipkart Lite PWA.
description: Flipkart Lite was the case study that put PWAs on the map for e-commerce. The current site no longer exposes its manifest to automated tools, so it carries the at-your-own-risk label.

name: DEV Community
tagline: A friendly blogging community for software developers.
description: dev.to was long a showcase open-source PWA known for instant page loads. Its current pages don't expose a manifest to our checker, so it's listed unverified.

name: Pocket Casts
tagline: A polished podcast player for the web.
description: Pocket Casts' web player syncs subscriptions and playback position across devices. The landing page doesn't expose the manifest to automated checks, so it's listed unverified.

name: TIDAL
tagline: Hi-fi music streaming in the browser.
description: TIDAL's web player streams its lossless catalog and installs for app-like listening. It blocks automated requests, so it can't be machine-verified.
